lines: Black oil, a heavy mechanical tread pressed into the ash, and a single shed rivet stamped with a kill-count and a name worn off.
Something armoured and very heavy stood in this doorway, then turned around and left without going inside. A Penitent Engine, by the tread; the warrior-monks do not abandon a route for nothing.
Whatever it heard in this chapel, it decided the chapel was someone else's problem and left. A regular cult does not scare iron off the road.
